observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to
immediately provide the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list
of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible
from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustments
of the same accounts previously taken-up in the books in order to present the
correct account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of gathering the collectibles for
both accounts manually.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of gathering the collectibles for both accounts manually. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2, page no. 30.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. determine the details of the discrepancies on the balances of CIB-LCCA and
Cash In Local Treasury between the accounting and treasury records and
subsequently effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
b. conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in
recording as required under Section 63(F) of the LTOM.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately provide the Municipal Accounting
Office an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the current and prior years
amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET
Receivables and/or adjustments of the same accounts previously taken-up in
the books in order to present the correct account balances in the financial
statements.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with the OlC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer to verify the net
differences of P(142,770.28) and P(227,338.99) between the recorded
balances of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts of the Municipality and their
reciprocal accounts Due to and Due from LGUs reflected in the books of the
Province;
b. instruct the Municipal Accountant to subsequently prepare the required
adjusting journal entries to present the correct balances of the said accounts
in the Municipality’s financial statements; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a periodic reconciliation of
accounts Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs in the Municipality books with the
corresponding reciprocal accounts in the books of the Province.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the liquidation reports of
the Fund Transfers to the DOH Region IV-A and initiate the return of
unused balance of the fund transfers and refund of disallowance to
the SAs BTr;
b. Focal person to ensure the immediate and proper implementation of projects
funded by the fund transfers from SAs;
c. henceforth, observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in
implementing projects funded by SAs so as to ensure that funds are utilized
and liquidated in a timely manner and that the services and benefits due to
the public are not hampered.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to expedite the completion of the inventory of PPE to
prove the existence of the Municipality’s properties and prepare the RRCPPE
in prescribed form, completely filled up the necessary data;
b. Property Custodian to prepare Property Cards for all PPE accounts in the
prescribed form and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain PPE
Ledger Cards for each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards
for land to substantiate the correctness of the general ledger balances for PPE
accounts; and
c. Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to conduct the
reconciliation of Accounting and GSO records. Any discrepancies found
should be investigated, and the necessary adjustments should be made
where the error was committed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Local Finance Committee to prepare Monthly Report of Fund
Utilization and Status of PPAs until the completion of the program reflecting
the correct information regarding the utilization and disbursements of
financial assistance as of reporting period and submit the same to the Audit
Team and to other offices pursuant to Item 4.4 of LBC No. 136; and
b) instruct the concerned official to post the said reports on the Municipality’s
website in order to properly disclose the related information regarding the
utilization and disbursements of the aforesaid financial assistance in public.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. make a representation to the concerned agencies on the disposition of the
fund balances of the completed program and projects;
b. require the Focal Person to ensure the immediate and proper implementation
of projects funded by the fund transfers from Source Agencies; and
c. direct he Municipal Accountant to initiate the reversion of the balances of the
municipal counterpart from the Trust Fund to General Fund book.

We strongly reiterated our
previous
recommendations that the
Municipal Mayor direct
the:
a. Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to
conduct the periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in
recording. Also, the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)
to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the Municipality’s
books for fair presentation of the CIB - LCCA and other affected accounts in the
financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The office of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are in
the process of reconciliation and are exerting extra efforts to comply with the
audit recommendations. Municipal Accountant drawn Journal Entry Voucher to
record all the valid reconciling items identified in the Bank Reconciliation
Statement.
observations.reason:
Still the two books Treasury and Accounting are not reconciled. are in
the process of reconciliation and are in the process of exerting extra page no. 29.
Reiterated in audit observation no.1, page no. 29.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Accountant with the complete and detailed cost segregation
of road components of completed road projects of the Municipality for CY
2018 up to present, prior to CY 2018 there was no inventory undertaken due
to failure of the Engineering office to provide the needed data;
b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed
road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering Office
report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of road
components; and
c. Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in
disclosing the data needed in disclosing the Notes to Financial Statements the
total Road Network System of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
The GSO was able to conduct inventory of completed road projects of the
Municipality for CY 2018 up to present, however for projects prior to CY
2018 no inventory was undertaken due to failure of the Engineering office to
provide the needed data. The Municipal Accountant will coordinate with the
Municipal Engineer to disclose the data needed in disclosing the Notes to
Financial Statements. The Municipal GSO is updating its record. The
Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian are in process of updating
their property card and the Inventory ledger card.
observations.reason: The inventory is incomplete. Not all road components were able to include in the actual physical count. The GSO is updating its record. Reiterated in audit observation no. 3 page no. 31

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with
the OIC-Provincial Accountant and Provincial Treasurer for the reconciliation of
Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can
be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s
financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and
were able to identify and adjust the difference between the two accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s property, prepare and submit the required report thereon, in
proper form, summarized according to type of property and properly
accomplished in all particulars. Any discrepancies between the inventory
report and the accounting and property records should be investigated.
Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was
committed;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete
and updated PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) and Property Cards (PCs),
respectively, in order to substantiate the balance of PPE accounts presented
in the financial statements and to facilitate reconciliation of their records; and
c. GSO to determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment was
issued, and issue the corresponding ARE for each property to pinpoint the
direct person accountable and responsible thereon.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer commented that
they will comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The GSO is updating its record. The Municipal Accountant is in process of updating the Inventory ledger card. Reiterated under audit observation no. 3 page no. 31

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MSWDO submit supporting documents showing
that the 14 recipients of the cash assistance are eligible beneficiaries of the
said program of the DSWD to substantiate the disbursements made and to
facilitate post audit of the same. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be
suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction:
The MSWDO submitted the supporting documents, thru the Municipal
Accountant showing the eligibility of the 14 recipients of the cash assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents, and the lacking supporting disbursement vouchers totaling
P2,858,165.00, to substantiate the disbursements made. Otherwise, the
transactions shall be suspended/ disallowed in audit; b) concerned municipal
personnel to submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the
submitted documents such as Purchase Request, Purchase Order,
Acceptance and Inspection Report and Property Acknowledgement Receipt;
c) Bids and Awards Committee to strictly comply with posting requirements of
GPPB Circular No. 2020-01 for information purposes and transparency of
procurement process of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. Concerned municipal
personnel submitted written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the
submitted documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. BAC, thru its BAC
Secretariat, to ensure compliance with the posting requirements of all
procurements in accordance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 RIRR
of RA No. 9184; b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit
Team the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements
made and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers to facilitate post audit of
the same. Otherwise, the said transactions shall be suspended in audit; and c.
concerned Municipal personnel to submit written explanation on the
deficiencies noted in the submitted documents such as Purchase Request,
Purchase Order, Acceptance and Inspection Report and Property
Acknowledgement Receipt.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the lacking supporting
documents and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. Concerned municipal
personnel submitted written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the
submitted documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Office along with the GAD Focal Point System to start data
collection and start establishing the GAD Database based on the format
required under RA No. 9710 and JMC 2016-1 in order to enable gender
analysis and gender responsive planning and programming.
observations.managementAction:
The MPDC/GAD Focal Person started data gathering and started the process
of establishing the GAD Database.
observations.reason: Still in the process of gathering the data needed to establish GAD Database based on the format required under RA No. 9710 JMC 2016-1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. BAC, thru its BAC
Secretariat, to ensure compliance with posting requirements of all procurements
in accordance with Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No.
9184; b. Municipal Accountant to immediately submit to the Audit Team the
lacking supporting documents to substantiate the disbursements made and the
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers to facilitate post audit. Otherwise, the
said transactions shall be suspended in audit; c. concerned Municipal
personnel to submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the
submitted documents; d. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the Report on
the Sources and Utilization on DRRMF are thorough reviewed and verified
before submitting to the Audit Team and other concerned agencies; and e.
Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amended LDRRMF Investment Plan
is submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC complied with the posting requirements of all procurement's
activities. The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the lacking
supporting documents and the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. The
concerned municipal personnel submitted written explanation on the
deficiencies noted in the submitted documents. The Municipal Accountant
ensured that the Report on the Sources and Utilization on DRRMF are review
and verified before submitting to the Audit Team and other concerned
agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to submit within the prescribed period all their required accounts
and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular
No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant submitted the required accounts and financial
reports to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: Per office records there were months that the submission of accounts and reports are not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Also.
necessary adjustment, if there’s any, should be effected at once to avoid
accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present
the correct balance of CIB - LCCA account in the financial statements; and

b. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all
valid reconciling items that require adjustment in the Municipality’s books
for fair presentation of the CIB - LCCA and other affected accounts in the
financial statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to
immediately provide the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list
of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible
from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustments
of the same accounts previously taken-up in the books in order to present the
correct account balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost
segregation of road components of completed road projects of the
Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, and to compute the
correct depreciation of its components and proper recognition of the same
in the books of accounts;

b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed
road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering
Office report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of
road components; and

c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
total Road Network System of the Municipality at year-end.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the
reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that
appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair
presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct
the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the
Municipality’s property, prepare and submit the required report thereon, in
proper form, summarized according to type of PPE and properly
accomplished in all particulars. Any discrepancies between the inventory
report and the accounting and property records should be investigated.
Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was
committed;

b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete
and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the
balance of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to
facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and

c. GSO to determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment was
issued, and issue the corresponding ARE for each property to pinpoint the
direct person accountable and responsible thereon.

We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Also, necessary adjustments, if there are any, should be effected at once to avoid accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the correct balance of CIB - LCCA account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: On-going reconciliation of records between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer and making corresponding correction entries on the difference found.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation. Reiterated in audit observation no. 1 page no. 30

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to immediately provide the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustments of the same accounts previously taken-up in the books in order to present the correct account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted certified list of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant disclosed that she did not accept and record the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasury because it only contains the amount due and collectible for CY 2020, excluding the uncollected RPT/SET. She also mentioned that the Municipal Treasury is in the process of updating and identifying the correct list of land and property owners and encoded the necessary data needed for the system being used in the Real Property Tax collections in order to come up with the complete correct certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be the basis in setting up RPT/SET Receivables. Reiterated in audit observation no. 2 page no. 32

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property, prepare and submit the required report thereon, in proper form, summarized according to type of PPE and properly accomplished in all particulars. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed; b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the balance of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and c. GSO to determine the officials/employees to whom the equipment was issued, and issue the corresponding ARE for each property to pinpoint the direct person accountable and responsible thereon.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property. The Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian will maintain a complete and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively. The Municipal Accountant had already prepared a complete Lapsing Schedule to support the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision made to Depreciation Expense.
observations.reason: The submitted RPCPPE was incomplete and not in the proper form. Reiterated in audit observation no. 5 page no. 36

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects of the Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, and to compute the correct depreciation of its components and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering Office report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of road components; and c. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total Road Network System of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer already submitted the prescribed form, the Municipal General Services Officer conducted the inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant will disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statement if the data needed is provided by the Engineering Office.
observations.reason: The submitted report is not in the prescribed form, contained only the names of project and the corresponding project cost. The complete details of Road Networks were not indicated such as the road name, road network ID, components description, cost, the dates constructed and conditions. The inventory report only includes the name and cost of the completed road projects and not the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of road components. Reiterated in audit observation no. 3 page no. 33

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from LGUs and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the Municipality’s financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant stated that the amount of P15,000.00 reflected in the Province’s records under Due from LGUs accounts was treated as outright expense in the Municipality’s books under the Medical Indigency Program. While the Due to LGUs account under the GF, the correct amount is P1,126,337.38 instead of P383,088.70, as mistakenly confirmed, thus leaving a difference of P163,052.54 if compared to the Province’s balance of P1,289,389.92. This matter was already communicated to the OIC-Provincial Accountant. Reiterated in audit observation no. 4 page no. 35

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 on the submission of contracts/POs, together with pertinent documents for timely and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team, so the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the perfected contracts/Purchase Orders (POs) including pertinent supporting documents were submitted to the Auditor for timely review and objective evaluation of the same transactions so that the Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the above-mentioned regulations, to facilitate the timely review/post-audit of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office are exerting extra efforts and implementing overtime services to enforce the timely and submission of all the accounts.
observations.reason: No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the audit recommendation. Reiterated in audit observation nos. 7, 8 page nos. 40 and 43

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the lacking supporting documents to attest the propriety and validity of the subject transactions; and ensure that all disbursements of the Municipality are duly supported with required documentations before processing the same for payment. Also ensure that all disbursement of the Municipality are duly supported with complete documentation before processing the same for payment to avoid audit suspensions and/or audit observation/disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Some of the disbursements are not supported with required documentation. The Municipal Accounting Office are exerting extra efforts up to the extent of adopting multi-tasking and overtime services to enforce the timely and submission of all the accounts.
observations.reason: Some of the disbursements are still lacking supporting documents. No reason was given by Management for the partial implementation of the recommendation. Reiterated in audit observation nos. 7 and 8 page nos. 40 and 43

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. concerned municipal officials to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided under the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 to invest in high-impact or development projects that would improve the socio-economic condition of the constituents; and b. Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund Proper to replenish the amount of the IRA of 20% DF utilized for project/s which do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. Otherwise, such transactions shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The municipal officials concerned will ensure that the programs, projects and activities chargeable to 20% Development Fund will improve the socio economic condition of the constituents. The Municipal Budget Officer commented that she had already refrain from using the 20% Development Fund for purposes other than those provided in DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
observations.reason: Some of the PPAs still doesn’t partake the nature of investments. Reiterated in audit observation no. 8 page no. 43

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to: a. ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned and that implementation thereof be in accordance with the approved GAD Plan and Budget to optimize the use of allocated funds; and b. strictly monitor the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to ensure that the GAD objectives are fully attained.
observations.managementAction: The Management had created projects, programs and activities that fully utilized the GAD Fund for the benefit of the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. MDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive LDRRMFIP which will be integrated in the AIP of the Municipality; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team in accordance with the period prescribed by the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO has already prepared a comprehensive LDRRMFIP and furnish the OCD and the DILG-MLGOO a copy of the same. The Municipal Accountant had furnished the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF through the LDRRMC and LDC to the Audit Team in accordance the period prescribed by the above cited regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We strongly reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct
periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure accuracy in recording. Also.
necessary adjustments. if there are any. should be effected at once to avoid
accumulation of the discrepancies between the two records and to present the
correct balance of CIB - LCCA account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in
coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to immediately provide the
Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the
current and prior years amount due and collectible from them as basis for
setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustments of the same accounts
previously taken-up in the books in order to present the correct account
balances in the financial statements.

We strongly reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal
Mayor direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory
of all the Municipality’s property, prepare and submit the required report
thereon to the Audit Team. Any discrepancies between the inventory report
and the accounting and property records should be investigated.
Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was
committed;

b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete
and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the
balance of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to
facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and

c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support
the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision made
to Depreciation Expense.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:

a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost
segregation of road components of completed road projects of the
Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and
proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts;

b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed
road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering
Office report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation
of road components;

c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with the General Services
Officer and Municipal Engineer reports on complete descriptions and cost
segregation of road components; and make the necessary adjustments in
order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account
in the financial statements; and

d. Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
total road network system of the Municipality at year-end.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for the
reconciliation of Due from and Due to LGUs accounts so that appropriate
adjustments can be made on the affected accounts for fair presentation in the
Municipality’s financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned officials to
strictly adhere with the above cited provision of COA Circular No. 2009-01 on
the submission of contracts/POs. together with pertinent documents for timely
and objective evaluation of the same by the Audit Team. so that the
Municipality could be immediately notified of any defects/deficiencies noted
thereon.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
to submit within the prescribed period all their accounts and financial reports
to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to the abovementioned regulations, to
facilitate timely review/post-audit of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team all the lacking
supporting documents to attest the propriety and validity of the subject
transactions; and
b) ensure that all disbursement of the Municipalityare duly supported with
required documentation before processing the same for payment to avoid
audit suspensions and/or audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a) Municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to the guidelines provided
under the DILG and DBMJMC No. 2017-1 to invest in high-impact or
development projects that would improve the socio-economic condition of
the constituents; and

b) Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate fund in the General Fund Proper to
replenish the amount of 20% DF utilized for project/s which do not partake
the nature of investment/capital expenditures. Otherwise, such
transactions shall be disallowed in audit. Further, refrain from using the
20% DF for purposes other than those provided in DILG and DBM JMC No.
2017-01.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property, prepare and submit the required report thereon to the Audit Team. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed; b) Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the balance of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and c) Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision made to Depreciation Expense.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted a inventory of all the Municipality’s property but failed to submit the required report.
The Municipal Accounting Office are exerting extra efforts to locate files/records of Plant, Property and Equipment acquired for the year 1999 and beyond in order to complete the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards
The Municipal Accountant prepared the Lapsing Schedule for the PPEs with available records
observations.reason:
The Inventory Committee was able to conduct inventory of the Municipality’s PPE but failed to submit the required report.
The Municipal Accounting Office disclosed that the Accounting Office are exerting extra effort to locate files/records of PPE acquired for the year 1999 and beyond in order to complete the PPELC as well as the Lapsing Schedule.
The Municipal Accountant was able to prepare lapsing schedule for PPE but incomplete

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the reconciliation of their respective records to determine the reason/s of the discrepancies between their balances and consequently, make the necessary corrections in order to ensure completeness of the recorded transactions as well as accuracy of the balances of Cash in Bank Accounts. Also, coordinate with the depository bank for the details of the unrecorded bank account to facilitate recording of the same in both Accounting and Treasury records.
observations.managementAction: For the CY 2019 the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer were able to record in their respective books the saving account (previously unrecorded account) deposited to the LBP (previously unrecorded account).
observations.reason: Cash in Bank account will be undertaken and deposited to the LBP make proper adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a) Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost segregation of road components of completed road projects of the Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; b) Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering Office report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of road components; c) Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with the General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer reports on complete descriptions and cost segregation of road components; and make the necessary adjustments in order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in the financial statements; and d) Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the total road network system of the Municipality at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer are able to provide Report on the projects implemented under road network has no detailed cost segregation for road network
The Municipal General Services Officer failed to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality
The Municipal Accountant promise to complete the recognition of Public-Infrastructure - Road Networks to make it 100 percent and disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements this Calendar Year 2020
observations.reason:
The Municipal Engineer disclosed that the road network report submitted by the Engineering Office for road network has no detailed cost segregation for road network
The Municipal General Services Officer failed to conduct inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality
Reiterated in observation no. 4, page no. 33

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to immediately provide the Municipal Accounting Office an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the current and prior years amount due and collectible from them as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables and/or adjustments of the same accounts previously taken-up in the books in order to present the correct account balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted list of taxpayers and the amount due (History) was not fully finished by the Assessor’s Office.
Encoding of Tax Declaration (History) was not fully finished by the Assessor’s Office.
They promise that they will exert all efforts to comply with the requirements.
Certified list of land and property owners were not yet fully established by Municipal Assessor’s Office
Reiterated in observation no. 3, page no. 32
observations.reason: Encoding of Tax Declaration (History) was not fully finished by the Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Local School Board provides allocations for the ECCD PPAS in the SEF Annual Budget as required under Section 7(b) of Republic Act. No. 10410, for the benefit of parents and children beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: For the CY 2019 the Municipal Local School Board provides allocation for the ECCD PPAS in the SEF Annual Budget

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to prioritize inclusion/creation of budgetary line item in the annual budget at least one per cent of the Municipality’s IRA exclusively for the strengthening and implementation of PPAs of the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2019 the Municipal Budget Officer prioritize the inclusion/creation of budget line item in the Annual Budget at least one percent of the Municipality’s IRA exclusively for the strengthening and implementation of PPAs of the LCPC;

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our prior years recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission of all their accounts and financial reports. In adherence to the provisions of Section 7.2 1(a) of COA Circular No. 2019-06.
observations.managementAction: The Management are exerting extra effort to submit the reports within the prescribed period. They also instructed the personnel in charge for the submission of reports. In adherence to the provisions of Section 7.2 1(a) of COA Circular No. 2019-06.
observations.reason: The Management commented that the main cause of the delay in the submission of reports is due to under staff of the Accounting Office but they are exerting extra effort to the extent of adopting multi-tasking to comply with the provision on the submission of the reports within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to: a) ensure that all the Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) are properly planned; and b) monitor strictly the implementation of the planned GAD PPAs to optimize the use of the allocated funds and to address all gender issues for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Follow up with the Rizal Provincial Government to fast track the GAD Center, Lobby with the Local Executive Officer and Sangguniang Bayan to ensure the creation and appointment of GAD Personnel. Monitoring of the implementation of GAD PPAs can be assigned when the creation/appointment of GAD personnel was approved.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the establishment of the LDRRMO in compliance with RA No. 10121 to ensure the safety and protection of the Municipality’s constituents during the times of disaster and emergency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor established the LDRRMO headed by Mr. Joselito F. Bernabe in compliance with RA No. 10121 to ensure the safety and protection of the Municipality’s constituents during the times of disaster and emergency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive prioritize the establishment of the LDRRMO in compliance with RA No. 10121 to ensure the safety and protection of the Municipality’s constituents during the times of disaster and emergency.
observations.managementAction: No action was yet taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Management stated they were not able to create all the mandatory positions due to Personnel Services constraint/budgetary requirement. They promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical
inventory of all the Municipality’s property and prepare and submit the
required report thereon to the Audit Team. Any discrepancies between the
inventory report and the accounting and property records should be
investigated. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error
was committed;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete
and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the
balances of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to
facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support
the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision made
for Depreciation Expense.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to intensify the reconciliation of their respective records to
determine the reason/s of the discrepancies between their records and
consequently, make the necessary corrections in order to ensure completeness
of the recorded transactions as well as the accuracy of the balances of Cash in
Bank accounts. Also, coordinate with the depository bank for the details of the
unrecorded bank account to facilitate recording of the same in both Accounting
and Treasury records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and
the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and detailed cost
segregation of road components of completed road projects of the
Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper
recognition of the same in the books of accounts;
b. Municipal General Services Officer to conduct inventory of all completed
road projects of the Municipality, based on the Municipal Engineering
Office report on the complete descriptions and detailed cost segregation of
road components; and
c. Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records with the General Services
Officer and Municipal Engineer reports on complete descriptions and cost
segregation of road components; and make the necessary adjustments in
order to present the correct balance of the Local Road Networks account in
the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately provide an updated certified list of taxpayers showing the
current/prior years amount due and collectible from them to the Municipal
Accounting Office as basis for setting-up of RPT/SET Receivables in order to
present their correct balances in the financial statements.

We reiterated that the Municipal Local School Board provides allocations for
the ECCD PPAs in the SEF Annual Budget as required under Section 7(b) of
Republic Act No. 10410, for the benefit of parents and children beneficiaries.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
prioritize inclusion/creation of budgetary line item in the annual budget at least one
percent of the Municipality’s IRA exclusively for the strengthening and
implementation of PPAs of the LCPC.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive
direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission of all
their accounts and financial reports, in adherence to the provisions of Section
7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006, to conduct timely review/post audit of
financial transactions of the Municipality.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical inventory of all the Municipality’s property and prepare and submit the required report thereon to the Audit Team. Any discrepancies between the inventory report and the accounting and property records should be investigated. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments where the error was committed;
b. Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian to maintain a complete and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, in order to substantiate the balances of PPE accounts presented in the financial statements and to facilitate reconciliation of their respective property records; and
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support the PPE balances in the financial statements as well as the provision made for Depreciation Expense.
observations.managementAction: The Property Custodian implemented the Physical Inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) for the year 2017 and the Property Custodian submitted their Inventory Report.
observations.reason:
The submitted inventory report was not consolidated and cannot be used to reconcile with the Accounting records.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 1, page 30

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the reconciliation of their respective records to determine the reason/s of the discrepancies between their records and consequently, make the necessary corrections in order to ensure completeness of the recorded transactions as well as the accuracy of the balances of Cash in Bank accounts;
b. Municipal Accountant, whenever book reconciling items occur in the preparation of BRS, to immediately investigate the same and secure the corresponding document as basis for recording in the books; and to strictly monitor the outstanding checks for possible maturing of the same into stale checks for immediate adjustment, to avoid accumulation of book reconciling items; and
c. Municipal Treasurer to transmit immediately to the Accounting Office the corresponding disbursement vouchers covering the noted unadjusted/unrecorded book reconciling items, 'Unrecorded Disbursements', for recording in the books of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
The Management is exerting effort in reconciling the Accounting and Treasury Office. The Management stated that corresponding disbursement vouchers were transmitted a week after to Accounting Office.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 2, page 31

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
1. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant with the list of completed road projects of the Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; and
2. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete and accurate inventory of all completed road projects of the Municipality as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office provided the list of road projects to the Municipal General Services Officer.
observations.reason:
The Municipal General Services Officer conducted the inventory for the road projects.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 3, page 33

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to come up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, encoding/ computerization/general revision of the certified and accurate list of taxpayers are still on-going.
Reiterated in audit observation no. 4, page 36

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BuB Focal Person to optimally utilize the funds for the intended purposes and to abide strictly to what were stipulated in the respective Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), most specially on the liquidation of funds with the concerned source agencies and the immediate return of unutilized funds to the source agencies.
observations.managementAction: Funds were utilized and the remaining balances were returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned Municipal Officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017 of DBM and DILG which provides guidelines and procedures in the implementation of the FY 2017 Local Government Support Fund-Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (LGSF-ADM) Program.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the approved contract and its pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Local School Board to allocate funds for the Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) programs, projects and activities in the Special Education Fund (SEF) Annual Budget in addition to the other local funds in accordance to Section 7 (b) of Republic Act No. 10410, otherwise known as the Early Years Act of 2013.
observations.managementAction: Allocation for the program was implemented and included in the plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation no. 5, page 37

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budgetary line item in the annual budget at least 1% of the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment exclusively for the strengthening and implementation of Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children.
observations.managementAction: Projects/Programs related to LCPC were implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in audit observation no. 6, page 38

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission of all their accounts and financial reports, in adherence to the provisions of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006, to conduct timely review/post audit of their accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the necessary documents/reports, however it was not within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC and other concerned offices submit all the required supporting documents enumerated above to the audit team through the Accountant to attest to the propriety and validity of the subject transactions. We also recommended that the Accountant ensures that all Agency financial transactions are duly supported with the required documentations before processing the same for payment to avoid audit suspensions and/or audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal Official implemented and submitted appeal memorandum for the subject disallowance. Appeal was received by COA Region IV-A on May 31, 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality utilize the unexpended balance of allocated funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and accordingly realign the funds to projects/program/activity undertakings which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially complied with the recommendation and implemented some projects.
observations.reason: Appropriation for the implemented projects were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality abide with DILG Memorandum No. 2012-73 in utilizing the 70% Mitigation Fund and maximize the utilization thereof to fully achieve the benefits of the programs, projects and activities funded therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially complied and the funds were utilized for some of the programs.
observations.reason: The projects were not fully implemented; some funds were utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the regulation on the timely remittance of trust collections to avoid delay in the processing of claims or forfeiture of benefits due to employees. The Municipal Accountant is also advised to facilitate the review and analysis of the non-moving account balances to determine the reason of such and effect the necessary adjustments. We further recommended that Management conduct reconciliation of these payable accounts and closely coordinate with the concerned agencies to complete the reconciliation so that Management can settle the subject obligations and accordingly establish the accuracy of the account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P427,198.95 Due to GSIS and P31,075.00 Due to Philhealth were remitted on January 2016. The remaining P123,229.92 and P4,860.00 were continuously being traced back for the existence.
observations.reason: The transactions were remitted except for certain balances which are being traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit to the Audit Team for review and evaluation, copies of perfected contracts and all documents forming part thereof within five working days from execution in order that the Management could be informed promptly of any defects/deficiencies found therein so that these could be immediately corrected and to avoid delays in the inspection of the projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management belatedly submitted copies of the documents and contracts to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Official-in-Charge of the water lily development project to turn-over the documents and financial information on the project under her custody to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and maintenance of special accounts through the use of subsidiary ledger for preparation of separate financial statements in the General Fund. Likewise, see to it that the Official-in-Charge of the project is properly designated and bonded.
observations.managementAction: The Management followed the recommendation and the Official-in-Charge turned over the documents and financial information on the project to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Budget Officer in coordination with the Local School Board to ensure the maximum and efficient utilization of the SEF for the priority programs and projects pertaining to the operations and maintenance of the public schools within the Municipality. Moreover, require the Budget Office and Accounting Office to conduct immediate reconciliation of their records in order to determine the cause of discrepancy amounting to (P438,957.00) and come up with an accurate and reliable financial data. The Accounting Office should be provided by the Budget Office with the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation / Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances to determine any adjustment/s to be effected in the SEF books.
observations.managementAction: The Management partially implemented the recommendation and utilized portions of the SEF for school operations.
observations.reason: Appropriation for the implemented programs/projects were not fully utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years' recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete and accurate physical
inventory of all the Municipality’s fixed assets and prepare and submit the
required report thereon. Any discrepancies between the inventory report
and the accounting and property records should be investigated and make
the necessary adjustments where the error was committed;

b. Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete PPE ledger cards for all
PPE accounts;

Municipal Property Custodian to maintain a complete and updated
property cards, as a counter check to the correctness of the accounting
records; and

<.

d. Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete Lapsing Schedule to support
the PPE balances in the Financial Statements as well as the provision
made for Depreciation Expense.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular
reconciliation of their respective records to ensure completeness of the
recorded transactions as well as the accuracy of the balance of Cash in
Bank account;

b. Municipal Accountant, whenever book reconciling item occur in the
preparation of BRS, to immediately investigate the same and secure the
corresponding document as basis for recording in the book; and to
strictly monitor the outstanding checks for possible maturing of the same
into stale check for immediate adjustment, to avoid accumulation of book
reconciling items, such as outstanding checks and stale checks, for correct
presentation of the balance of Cash in Bank account in the financial
statements; and

¢. Municipal Treasurer to transmit immediately to the Accounting Office
the corresponding disbursement vouchers covering the noted
unadjusted/unrecorded book  reconciling items, ‘Unrecorded
Disbursements’, for recording in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

1. Municipal Engineering Office to provide the Municipal General Services
Officer and the Municipal Accountant a list of completed road projects of
the Municipality to facilitate the conduct of inventory, reconciliation and
proper recognition of the same in the books of accounts; and

2. Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete and accurate inventory of all
completed road projects of the Municipality as required under COA
Circular No. 2015-008.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to
submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers
showing the name of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from
each of them at the start of each year to serve as basis in setting-up the
Real Property Tax/Special Education tax Receivables, in compliance with
Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and

b. Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer come
up with the details of the accounts showing the complete list of the
taxpayers and the amounts collectible from them in order to reflect a
reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BuB Focal
Person to optimally utilize the funds for the intended purposes and to abide strictly
to what were stipulated in the respective Memorandum of Agreement (MOA), most
specially on the liquidation of funds with the concerned source agencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
Municipal Officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017 of DBM and DILG which provides
guidelines and procedures in the implementation of the FY 2017 Local Government
Support Fund-Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (LGSF-ADM) Program.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Local
School Board to allocate funds for the Early Childhood Care and Development
(ECCD) programs, projects and activities in the Special Education Fund (SEF)
Annual Budget in addition to the other local funds in accordance to Section 7 (b) of
Republic Act No. 10410, otherwise known as the Early Years Act of 2013 for the
benefit of parents and children beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to provide budgetary line item in the annual budget at least 1% of
the Municipality’s Internal Revenue Allotment exclusively for the strengthening and
implementation of Programs, Projects and Activities of the Local Council for the
Protection of Children.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the timely submission
of all their accounts and financial reports, in adherence to the provisions of Section
7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006, to conduct timely review/post audit of their
accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Budget Officer in coordination with the Local School Board to ensure the maximum and efficient utilization of the SEF for the priority programs and projects pertaining to the operations and maintenance of the public schools within the Municipality.   Moreover, require the  Budget Office and Accounting Office to conduct immediate reconciliation of their records in order to determine the cause of discrepancy amounting to (P438,957.00) and come up with an accurate and reliable financial data.  The Accounting Office should be provided by the Budget Office with the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation / Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations and Balances to determine any adjustment/s to be effected in the SEF books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer are still conducting reconciliation of records to trace the discrepancy.
observations.reason: No reason given by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’ recommendations that the Local Chief
   Executive gives utmost importance   being  able  to  present  valid  and
   reliable PPE account balances in the financial statements by:


        a. directing the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
           inventory of all the  Agency’s  fixed  assets  and  prepare  and
           submit the required report thereon.  Any  discrepancies  between
           the inventory report and the  accounting  and  property  records
           should be  investigated and make the necessary adjustments where
           the error was committed;


        b. requiring the Accountant to maintain PPE ledger  cards  for  all
           PPE accounts;


        c.  directing  the  Municipal  Property  Custodian  to  maintain  a
           complete and updated property cards, as a counter check  to  the
           correctness of the accounting records; and

        d. requiring the Municipal Accountant to provide a Complete Lapsing
           Schedule to support the PPE balances in the Financial Statements
           as well as the provision made for Depreciation Expense.

We recommended that the concerned municipal  officials  adjust  the
   identified reconciling items in the books of the Municipality to come up
   with the correct account balance of the Cash  in  bank  account  in  the
   books.


         We also recommended that the  Honorable  Municipal  Mayor  requires
   both the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer  to  reconcile
   at the end of each month their bank account records.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation  that  the  Municipal
   Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor,  submit  to  the
   Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the name
   of the taxpayers and amount due and collectible from each of them at the
   start of each year to serve as basis in  setting-up  the  Real  Property
   Tax/Special Education tax Receivables, in compliance with Section 20  of
   the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.


         We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant  in  coordination
   with the Municipal Treasurer come up with the details  of  the  accounts
   showing the complete list of the taxpayers and the  amounts  collectible
   from them in order to reflect a reliable RPT and SET Receivable accounts
   balances.

We recommended that the BAC and other concerned offices submit  all
   the required supporting documents enumerated  above  to  the  audit  team
   through the Accountant to attest to the propriety  and  validity  of  the
   subject transactions.


        We also recommended that the Accountant  ensures  that  all  Agency
   financial   transactions   are   duly   supported   with   the   required
   documentations before processing the same  for  payment  to  avoid  audit
   suspensions and/or audit disallowances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  instructs  the
   concerned municipal officials to expedite the implementation of the  BUB
   programs and projects, with the end in view of providing  immediate  and
   adequate social and livelihood services projects for the benefit of  the
   intended beneficiaries.


         Also, we recommended that the LCE directs the BUB-Focal  Person  to
   provide  the  Municipal  Accounting  Office  and  the  Audit  Team,  the
   necessary documents to support  her  claim  that  certain  BuB  projects
   reported to be unimplemented were already fully implemented.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  stop  approving
   payments of honoraria to personnel engaged in the ALS  program  if  such
   are charged against the  SEF,  as  these  are  not  among  the  eligible
   expenses chargeable thereto.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive:


       • Instructs the Bids and Awards Committee to provide an invitation to
         COA and at least two (2) other observers  and  to  strictly  comply
         with the requirement of invitation in all stages of the procurement
         to be able to achieve transparency in procurement  as  intended  by
         law; and


       • Require the BAC Secretariat to be keen in posting of  documents  in
         the PhilGEPS and in the Municipality’s website as required by  R.A.
         9184, to promote transparency to  the  public  on  the  procurement
         process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal
   Accountant  to  immediately  submit  to  the  Audit  Team  the  CY  2016
   transaction vouchers still in their custody  and  strictly  enforce  the
   timely submission of  all  their  accounts  and  financial  reports,  in
   adherence to the provisions of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular 2009-006
   and Section 6.03, 6.04 and 6.05 of COA Circular No. 95-006, to allow the
   Audit Team to conduct timely review/post audit of their accounts.

         Also, Management  should  address  the  lack  of  manpower  in  the
   Municipal Accounting Department.

We  strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive compel the Treasurer/ Inventory Committee to conduct complete inventory-taking of the Municipality’s properties and prepare the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment which should be reconciled with the property records of the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Unit and the PPE ledger cards of the Accounting Office.                                                                                                                                                                                     Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Unit be required to maintain property cards and the Municipal Accountant to update the property ledger cards and  take up donated property in the books of accounts to facilitate reconciliation with physical inventory report and the records of the Municipal Treasurer/Property Unit.  
Lastly, we recommended that the Management consider the creation of the position of a General Services Officer, although optional on the municipal level per RA 7160, to remedy the inefficiency  in the property records, maintenance and disposal of properties, preparation and submission of the RPCPPE of the Municipality and to perform other functions of the GSO as enumerated under Section 490 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Agency submitted their RPCPPE. The OIC-Property Custodian has started maintaining property cards and the Accounting Office maintained a columnar worksheet for each PPE item with details per property, and are in the processs of preparing the property ledgers.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no.1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to verify  and  evaluate  the reconciling  items  pertaining  to  the  difference  amounting    to (P77,000.03) and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to establish  the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts for all funds presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant made the necessary adjustments for the affected accounts  and prepared the JEVs for the adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Reiterated in finding no.2

observations.recommendation: We  strongly reiterated that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Assessor to exert extra effort to complete the cleansing and updating the assessment records and furnish the Municipal Treasurer with the schedule of assessed values of each property per taxpayer.   Thereafter, coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for proper reconciliation of their respective records.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer upon the Municipal Assessor’s completion of its cleansing come up with the certified list of taxpayers showing the real property taxes due the Municipality to provide the Municipal Accountant with a complete and accurate list of taxpayers with the amounts due and collectible as the correct basis in setting up the correct Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year.  The need to recognize the actual RPT and SET Receivables from taxpayers is necessary in order to fairly present the actual financial position of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal  Assessor furnished the Municipal Treasurer with a list of Real Property Taxpayers, however, it was still inaccurate. Cleansing and updating of the list of taxpayers are still on going.
observations.reason:
No reason given by the Management.

Reiterated in finding no.3

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require  the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to make a detailed review of the Subsidy from Other Funds and Subsidy to Other Funds accounts and to determine the cause/s of the unreconciled amount.  Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/ corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise due care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of the reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted detailed review of the subject accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Human Resource Management Officer to support the payments made for the services rendered by Job Order employees with duly verified Accomplishment Reports to justify the hiring of job orders by the Municipal Government.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant record expenses in the appropriate accounts to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.  Moreover, the Budget Officer is advised to refrain from charging salary expenses of Job Order Employees to Other MOOE accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the   Accomplishment Reports needed to support the payments made for the services rendered by the Job Order employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to immediately utilize the amount of P4,877,046.00 for its intended purposes to ensure that the projects would benefit its constituents.  In case the Municipal Government decided to use the fund for other purpose, the approval of the source of fund should be sought first to ensure its propriety.  Otherwise, the fund should be returned to the National Government in order that it may be made available for other priority programs or projects of said government agencies. Also, we recommended that Management explain/justify for the non-implementation of the said projects
observations.managementAction: Out of the P4,877,046.00 unutilized funds from the National Government Agencies, P4,727,046.00 was utilized in CY 2016. Also, the amount of P150,000.00 was returned to the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned Municipal Officials to immediately facilitate the remittance of the unexpended balances of fund transfer to the source agency as required under Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 2994, in order that it may be made available to other government agencies needing funds to finance their priority projects/ programs.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance amounting to P158,509.84 which were unimplemented were returned to the Bureau of Treasury and concerned National Govt. Agencies. The remaining balance were implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the regulation on the timely remittance of trust collections to avoid delay in the processing of claims or forfeiture of benefits due to the employees.  The Municipal Accountant is also advised to facilitate the review and analysis of the non-moving account balances to determine the reasons for such and effect the necessary adjustments.

We further recommended that Management conduct reconciliation of these payable accounts and closely coordinate with the concerned agencies to complete the reconciliation so that Management can settle the subject obligations and accordingly establish the accuracy of the account balances reflected in the financial statements.
