observations.managementAction: The amount of P427,198.95 Due to GSIS and P31,075.00 Due to Phlhealth were remitted on January 2016. The remaining balance of P123,229.92 and P4,860.00 were continuously being traced back for the reason of existence.
observations.reason: The Municipal  Accountant explained that these were accumulated from previous years dating back from 1998 and they encountered difficulty in tracing back the reason for its existence.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit to the Audit Team for review and evaluation, copies of perfected contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five working days from execution in order that the Management could be informed promptly of any defects/deficiencies found therein so that these could be immediately corrected and to avoid delays in the inspection of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Management belatedly submitted copies of the contracts to the Team.
observations.reason: No reason given by the the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Official-in-Charge of the water lily development project to turn-over the documents and financial information on the project under her custody to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording and maintenance of special accounts through the use of subsidiary ledger for preparation of separate financial statements in the General Fund.  Likewise, see to it that the Official-in-Charge of the project is properly designated and bonded.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the bond of the focal person was submitted to the Audit Team on April 17, 2017.
observations.reason: The Focal person still did not submit to the Accountant the financial data needed to prepare a separate financial records for the said project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the required reports including the disbursement vouchers, collection reports together with its supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.  Henceforth, she should determine and find solutions as to the causes of delay in the submission of the said reports.  She should comply strictly with the provisions of the said regulations on the period within which to submit the above reports and all their supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate the timely examination and verification of the same.  Consequently, deficiencies/ errors as well as irregularities if any, can be detected immediately enabling the concerned municipal officials to take appropriate action without delay.  This will expedite also the preparation and submission by the Audit Team of the reports required by stakeholders and government agencies needed for their decision making.
observations.managementAction: Weekly  submission by the Municipal Treasurer of collection and disbursement reports to the Accounting Dept. was being implemented.
observations.reason:
Management stated that the delayed submission of accounts was due to the lack of manpower in the Mun. Accounting Department.

Reiterated in finding no.8

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management cause the submission of a complete list of all on-going projects/ programs/activities and those that are to be implemented for the year 2015 and the ensuing years, with all the necessary information.  Impose strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 by providing the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of projects/ programs/activities in order to allow timely validation of the same.  Otherwise, administrative disciplinary action as provided under Section 127 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed.  This will also enable the Audit Team to prepare and submit the reports required by government agencies and stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: During CY 2016, the list of all on-going projects were submitted  to the Audit Team. The list, however, did not provide the information as to the status whether it was still on going or completed, date started, name of contractor and other important details.
observations.reason: No reason given by the the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose strict compliance with the provisions of Memorandum Circular No. 65 of the Office of the President dated June 14, 1988  and CSC Circular No. 27 on the retention/extension of service of compulsory retirees.  

We further recommended that the Management be directed to support the services rendered by the retirees with the approval of the CSC on the required request for extension of services of compulsory retirees.  

Also, it is recommended that Municipality explain and justify the retention of services of the said employees who have reached the compulsory retirement age of 65 years.
observations.managementAction: Management sent a query to CSC regarding extension of term of co-terminuous appointee who reaches compulsary age of retirement. The Director of the CSC Regional Office responded and explained that a co-terminous employee who reaches the age of 65 is considered automatically extended in the service until the expiry date of his/her appointment or until his/her services are earlier terminated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive impose compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656, as amended, to insure all properties of the Municipality with the Property Insurance Fund of GSIS, so that in case of loss or destruction, the Municipality could recover the cost of these assets.  Moreover, see to it that funds for the purpose are included in the annual budget of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management had insured  two (2) municipal vehicles and the transport terminal and public market  and  had included in the annual budget funds for insurance of properties.
observations.reason: No reason provided by the Management.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented as planned for the benefit of its constituents.  It is also recommended that the Local Chief Executive  direct  the Chief of the Municipal Planning Development Council to immediately look into the unimplemented developmental projects included in the Annual Investment Plan and identify causes of non-implementation and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement the same.   Likewise, instruct the Local Development Council to prepare a detailed Annual Investment Plan showing among others, program/ project/activity description; implementing office; starting date; completion date; the source of fund and most especially the expected outputs in quantified terms so that lump sum appropriation with PPA described in generic terms to be funded from the 20% Development Fund be given priority in the implementation.  Moreover, instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very precise in evaluating the timely implementation of each project.  

We further recommended that the Municipal Development Council should review, assess and recommend programs, projects and activities that may be funded out of the 20% Development Fund in compliance with DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 to optimize the utilization of the said fund.
observations.managementAction: During CY 2016, the Agency had implemented projects charged to the CY 2015 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason:
Management had encountered legal impediments in the implementation of projects specifically on the land acquisition.

Reiterated in finding no.9

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to plan disaster risk reduction management programs/ projects/activities to be funded out of the previous years unexpended balance of LDRRMF to attain maximum utilization of the fund and the realization of the Municipality’s ultimate goals and objectives of protecting the lives and properties of its constituents during calamities.
observations.managementAction: During CY 2016, part of the unused CY 2015 LDRRMF was utilized.
observations.reason:
No reason given by the Management.

Reiterated in finding no.10.1

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management enforce immediate settlement of the audit suspensions totaling P20,100,000.00,in accordance with Section 9.4 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.  Henceforth, ensure that government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before processing claims and effecting payments to minimize suspensions and disallowances in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management has filed Memorandum Appeals for the Notices of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for the implementation of GAD Plan.  Likewise, direct the GAD Focal Point Officer to include in the GAD Plan specific programs that address the gender concerns of its constituents with specific targets for easy monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments to ensure the effective attainment of GAD objectives and efficient utilization of the resources.   Also, we recommended that the GAD Focal Point Officer justify their failure to undertake the two GAD programmed activities
observations.managementAction: The required 5% of the total budget appropriation was allocated for GAD related programs, projects and activites.
